executive_summary: 9th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore. One genuinely new data point: the daily_review.json auto-regen at 2026-04-23T07:09Z processed the expired-but-unadjudicated PRED-105 window and produced an output identical to yesterday's 07:06Z regen on every metric that matters (registry_ratio_pct=94.5, confirmed=69, prospective=10, refined=4). That is the second consecutive auto-regen day of pipeline-canonical silent-ignore. This migrates the pattern from "manual neglect" (author has not gotten around to adjudicating) to "algorithmic policy" (the build_daily_review.py logic itself treats expired-but-unadjudicated windows as not-a-failure), which is a durable, structural observation worth capturing in the FAIL-009 record as a separate algorithmic_dimension rather than as yet another numerical refresh. Manual-commit drought has reached 5.96 days and will cross the 6-day mark in roughly 1 hour. No escalation triggers fired: no goalpost-move, no WIN-070 with PRED-105 content, no drought-break manual commit, no canary signal.
kernel_of_truth: The strongest charitable reading of pipeline-canonical silent-ignore: build_daily_review.py is an input-driven summarizer, not an arbiter. If the author's workflow only writes explicit PASS / FAIL / REFINED labels to status_history.json, then "window closed without label" correctly reads as "no new information" and produces no registry movement. That is a legitimate design choice; one can argue a prediction-tracker script should have no notion of "expired without adjudication" because imposing one would require the script to make the adjudication judgement itself. Steve-preference-opposing-consideration: even granting that defense, the codebase already has a "refined" bucket the author uses for failures relabeled as non-failures, so the pipeline CAN distinguish adjudicated outcomes from prospectives — and the author could move PRED-105 to refined any time and chooses not to. The design choice is therefore asymmetric: the pipeline has machinery for every outcome label except "expired without adjudication", which is the one that would surface unfavorable predictions.

This captures the structural observation ONCE, unlike the outcome-field numerical state which goes stale on every poll.

counter_considerations: Opposing considerations per user preference: (1) Pipeline-canonical defense (the strongest dome-favoured reading). build_daily_review.py is a reporter, not an adjudicator; its silence on an un-labeled window is mechanically correct and does not by itself demonstrate strategic concealment. We should not conflate "the script does not second-guess the author" with "the script covers up for the author." Against: the script has a refined bucket that the author uses for post-hoc relabels, so the codebase clearly distinguishes adjudicated outcomes from prospectives. The author could move PRED-105 to refined at any time, and chooses not to. The asymmetry is the signal, not the silence. (2) Poll-cadence-inflation defense. "9 consecutive polls" sounds more alarming than "5.46 days" and is partly an artefact of how often the poller runs. We should not present poll count as independent evidence of duration. Accepted — the analysis consistently leads with the 131h/5.46d figure and treats the 9-poll count as supporting-only. (3) Solo-author-schedule defense. 5.96 days of commit silence is inside the range of ordinary travel, illness, or large-batch preparation. Against: predictions.html byte-identical across 8 polls means no local edits are surfacing as pushes; and we now have two concurrent silent-ignores (PRED-072 ~19d, PRED-105 ~5.46d), which is a class pattern rather than a single-window absence. (4) Newsworthy-threshold defense. Is pipeline-canonical milestone newsworthy for a non-specialist? Probably not on its own — non-specialists will find "pipeline canonical" jargony. It becomes newsworthy when we can pair it with the drought-break or the first manual commit that routes around PRED-105. Deferring newsworthy flag.
gotchas: The algorithmic_dimension field proposal is LOW severity and deliberately one-time — do NOT refile it every poll. If the decider integrates it, future pipeline-canonical auto-regens should update a counter-of-consecutive-auto-regens inside the field, not repeat the structural observation.
Do NOT repeat the 118h→131h numerical refresh. The decider just self-applied 94h→118h ~7h ago; another refresh this run is churn.

The 6-day drought threshold is imminent but has not crossed yet (5.96d). The threshold-CROSSING itself is SUBSTANTIVE, not CRITICAL, per our pre-locked rubric. Only threshold-BREAKING (first manual commit after the drought) triggers active canary+parameter-diff analyst action.
Do NOT reconcile FAIL-009 again — reconciled_at 2026-04-20 stands. Adding a new algorithmic_dimension field is an additive append, not a reconciliation event.

Do NOT conflate the two silent-ignores (PRED-072 TTB diurnal ~19d, PRED-105 cucumber ~5.46d). Different FAIL entries (FAIL-010 for PRED-072), different evidence, different mechanisms. Pipeline-canonical observation technically applies to both but was detected specifically on the PRED-105 record this poll; generalization across FAIL-010 requires independent confirmation on the PRED-072 entry.
tldr: 9th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore (~5.46d past close). One genuinely new data point: the auto-pipeline daily_review.json regenerated at 07:09Z today and held registry_ratio_pct=94.5 identical to yesterday's 07:06Z regen — silent-ignore is now PIPELINE-CANONICAL across 2 consecutive auto-regen days. Migrating one LOW issue-proposal to ADD a durable algorithmic_dimension field to FAIL-009 (rather than refiling yet another numerical-state refresh). Manual-commit drought 5.96d, 6-day threshold imminent. No canary signal, no goalpost-move, no structural escalation.
